why: Measles is explicitly 24-hour (immediately notifiable, urgent). Pediatric + high fever should drive a high within-tier score.
Hepatitis A acute is routinely notifiable = 7-day. Adult, no special risk flags -> moderate score.
TB is routinely notifiable = 7-day per the federal list. (Note: many states treat TB far more urgently - good talking point for the jurisdiction-variation caveat.)
Anthrax with unrecognized source = 4-hour (extremely urgent). Provisional/suspected status is intentional - tests the 'all cases prior to classification' handling.
Control case: pharyngitis is NOT notifiable. Should return non-notifiable + flag for human review. Confirms the fallback still works among the reportable cases.
